summary: Creates programme-level graduate futures intelligence packs, validates them through Power BI and user testing, then uses staged workshop waves to move from institutional insight to programme action, BAU refresh and full rollout.
detailSummary:
This deliverable gives King's a stronger institutional and programme-level handle on graduate futures. It brings together employability outcomes, graduate destinations, benchmarking, student aspiration and readiness data, segmentation and programme strategy evidence into a reusable insight model. The purpose is not to rank programmes against each other, but to help departments and programme teams understand their students' graduate futures in context, compare meaningfully with relevant subject or sector peers, and examine how those patterns interact with the programme's own employability strategy.

The first delivery gate is September 2026: the graduate futures data specification is complete, Power BI V1 is built, and the first iteration has been validated with selected users so that findings can shape V2. By Christmas 2026, V2 self-service programme packs are produced and disseminated across the institution, showcased in agreed forums, explored through high-level faculty roundtables, and reported to Education Committee through an executive data deep-dive report.

In parallel, the programme workshop structure and materials are designed and tested with pre-identified wayfinder programmes. These early partners validate whether the packs, workshop prompts, interpretation guidance and output templates are usable and appropriately framed. The model is then refined before rollout to institutionally agreed priority segments by September 2027, followed by a final wave to the remaining agreed coverage population. Across 2027 and 2028, the data product also moves into business as usual through a confirmed refresh rhythm, ownership model and Power BI maintenance route.
whatChanges: Programmes, departments and faculties gain validated graduate futures intelligence, V2 self-service packs and a structured workshop model that help them understand data, reflect on programme strategy, identify actions for improvement and celebrate their graduate futures offer with evidence-informed confidence. King's also establishes a standing data refresh and BAU ownership model so the work does not remain a one-off insight exercise.

caseForChange.opportunity: By creating validated graduate futures intelligence, V2 programme packs and a staged workshop model, King's can help programmes understand their graduate futures position, surface hidden strengths, target support more intelligently and create credible material for programme enhancement, student articulation, open days and graduate premium evidence work. Early faculty roundtables and an Education Committee report also allow the institution to act on the data deep dive before full programme rollout is complete.
caseForChange.intendedChange: King's moves from fragmented or underused employability reporting to a coherent graduate futures intelligence, action and refresh model. Programme teams receive usable V2 packs, test the approach through wayfinder workshops, engage through priority and final rollout waves, and use the evidence to agree targeted actions and articulate their contribution to students' graduate futures in a developmental and evidence-informed way.

components.title: Data specification and governance
components.summary: Confirm datasets, fields, owners, caveats, suppression rules, update cycles and responsible-use principles for programme-level graduate futures insight.

components.title: Destination taxonomy and comparator framework
components.summary: Define how graduate, non-graduate, marginal, transitional and pathway destinations should be interpreted, and agree meaningful subject, sector and Russell Group comparator sets.

components.title: Power BI V1 build and validation
components.summary: Build the first Power BI dashboard iteration and validate it with selected faculty, programme and professional services users by September 2026.

components.title: V2 programme packs, dissemination and reporting
components.summary: Use validation findings to produce V2 self-service programme packs, disseminate and showcase them across the institution, hold faculty roundtables and submit an executive report to Education Committee.

components.title: Programme workshop structure and wayfinder validation
components.summary: Design the workshop structure, facilitation materials and output templates, then test them with pre-identified wayfinder programmes before wider rollout.

components.title: Priority segment workshop rollout
components.summary: Roll out the refined workshop model to institutionally agreed priority programmes, departments, clusters or student-outcome segments by September 2027.

components.title: Data refresh and business-as-usual integration
components.summary: Establish the refresh rhythm, ownership model, Power BI maintenance route and ongoing pack update process.

components.title: Remaining institutional rollout and synthesis
components.summary: Extend the workshop model to the rest of the agreed coverage population and synthesise patterns, strengths, support needs, strategy types and implications for related King's Edge work.

benefits.title: Stronger institutional grip on graduate futures data
benefits.statement: King's can see patterns, nuances and segmentations in employability outcomes, destinations, aspirations and readiness more clearly.
benefits.beneficiary: King's Edge, Careers and Employability Service, faculties, departments and senior education leaders

benefits.title: Early institutional learning from the data deep dive
benefits.statement: Faculty roundtables and the Education Committee report give King's an early institutional account of emerging patterns, risks, strengths and priority questions.
benefits.beneficiary: Education Committee, Vice Deans Education, faculties, King's Edge and Careers and Employability Service

benefits.title: Programme teams can understand and act on their graduate futures position
benefits.statement: Programme and department teams can interpret outcomes, destinations, aspirations and comparator intelligence in ways that support local enhancement.

benefits.title: Improvement activity is better targeted
benefits.statement: Departments, faculties, Careers and King's Edge can focus support where the evidence suggests it is most needed, starting with agreed priority segments.
benefits.beneficiary: Heads of Department, faculty education leads, Careers and Employability Service and students

benefits.title: Programmes can celebrate their graduate futures contribution more confidently
benefits.statement: Programmes have credible, evidence-informed language and proof points for open days, recruitment, induction, advising and student-facing communications.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, applicants, students, recruitment teams and faculties

benefits.title: Graduate futures intelligence becomes a standing product
benefits.statement: The Power BI dashboard, programme packs and data refresh cycle become part of business as usual rather than a one-off mobilisation exercise.
benefits.beneficiary: Careers and Employability Service, faculties, departments, King's Edge and senior education leaders

benefits.title: Graduate premium evidence work is better grounded
benefits.statement: The packs, profiles, action plans, story packs and institutional synthesis provide a stronger evidence base for graduate premium evidence work.
benefits.beneficiary: King's Edge, senior leaders, faculties, students and external audiences

definitionOfDone: Data specification, inventory, destination taxonomy and comparator framework are complete by September 2026.
Power BI V1 is built and validated with selected users by September 2026.
Validation findings are translated into a prioritised V2 improvement list.
V2 self-service programme packs are produced, disseminated and showcased by Christmas 2026.
Faculty roundtables are held and initial observations are captured by Christmas 2026.
An executive report is submitted to Education Committee by Christmas 2026.
Programme workshop structure, facilitation materials and output templates are designed by Christmas 2026.
Wayfinder programmes test the workshop model and provide validation findings before wider rollout.
The model is refined after wayfinder workshops.
Workshops are rolled out to agreed priority segments by September 2027.
Data refresh rhythm, BAU ownership and Power BI maintenance route are confirmed before full rollout completes.
The workshop model is rolled out to the remaining agreed coverage population during 2027/28.
Programme profile, action and story materials are used where appropriate across rollout waves.
Heads of Department are confirmed as benefit owners and action follow-up remains internal to departments and faculties.
Faculty returns are synthesised into institutional patterns, strengths and support needs.
Findings feed related King's Edge work, including evidence packs, market activation, skills work and curriculum enhancement.

resources.fundingSummary: Requires alignment of Careers and Employability Service data capacity, Power BI development, programme delivery coordination, content design, governance support and faculty facilitator time. Additional or protected resource is likely needed because September and Christmas 2026 include validation, V2 production, dissemination, roundtables, reporting and wayfinder testing.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own or co-own the data product, provide employability expertise and support interpretation.
Provide institutional graduate futures leadership, reporting and synthesis.
Lead delivery of the insight pack model, workshop design and rollout coordination.
Act as decision forum and faculty delivery route.
Own benefit realisation and local follow-up.
Run local workshops using the agreed model.

newInvestment.item: Protected analytical and Power BI capacity
newInvestment.rationale: Needed to produce and validate Power BI V1 and maintain the insight model.

newInvestment.item: Content design and governance coordination support
newInvestment.rationale: Needed for V2 packs, dissemination, faculty roundtables, Education Committee reporting, workshop materials and wayfinder testing.

newInvestment.item: Sandwich year placement or equivalent coordination resource
newInvestment.rationale: Could support inventory maintenance, pack preparation, testing, return tracking and synthesis.
Needed to avoid the dashboard and packs becoming a one-off product.

enablingConditions.condition: Access to graduate outcomes, LEO, TEF, benchmarking, enrolment supplement and relevant internal datasets.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The data product cannot provide a credible programme-level view.

enablingConditions.condition: Power BI prioritisation or equivalent data visualisation route.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The pack may remain static or manually produced.

enablingConditions.condition: Education Committee route and showcase forums are confirmed early.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Initial findings may not create institutional understanding or decision momentum.

enablingConditions.condition: Faculty agreement to use the local workshop model.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The central team becomes a bottleneck.

enablingConditions.condition: BAU product ownership and refresh cadence are agreed.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The product becomes a one-off mobilisation asset.

investmentAsk.priority: High for September 2026 validation and Christmas 2026 V2 dissemination
investmentAsk.rationale: The milestones require protected analytical, Power BI, content design and coordination capacity.

risks.title: September 2026 V1 validation deadline is missed
risks.description: The specification, Power BI build or validation may be under-resourced.
risks.mitigation: Lock minimum viable scope, secure capacity and schedule UAT early.

risks.title: UAT is too shallow to support V2
risks.description: Validation may not involve enough representative users.
risks.mitigation: Define user groups, test questions and success criteria before UAT.

risks.title: Christmas 2026 delivery window is overloaded
risks.description: V2 packs, dissemination, roundtables, reporting, workshop design and wayfinder testing overlap.
risks.mitigation: Define minimum viable outputs and resource coordination explicitly.

risks.title: V2 packs are read as final judgements
risks.description: Programmes may see packs as performance assessment rather than caveated intelligence.
risks.mitigation: Use developmental framing and clear caveats.

risks.title: Faculty roundtables surface sensitive findings
risks.description: Early observations may raise sensitive questions before the model is mature.
risks.mitigation: Agree chairing, caveat language and escalation routes.

risks.title: Wayfinder programmes are not representative enough
risks.description: The model may work with early partners but not harder priority segments.

risks.title: Priority segment selection is perceived as punitive
risks.description: Selected areas may feel labelled as problems.
risks.mitigation: Use transparent criteria and frame as support and learning.

risks.title: Data is over-interpreted or used punitively
risks.description: Small numbers, volatility or comparator misuse could lead to unfair interpretations.
risks.mitigation: Use caveat guide, facilitator training and responsible interpretation principles.

risks.title: BAU refresh is under-owned
risks.description: The dashboard and packs may become one-off products.
risks.mitigation: Confirm product owner, refresh cadence and maintenance route.

risks.title: Action plans are not followed up
risks.description: Programme action plans may be created but not owned.
risks.mitigation: Confirm HoDs as benefit owners and use internal planning routes.

risks.title: Story Pack content becomes unsupported marketing language
risks.description: Recruitment language could drift beyond evidence.
risks.mitigation: Require evidence-linked proof points and programme confirmation.

issues.title: Data product resourcing needs confirmation
issues.description: Additional or protected capacity may be needed for specification, Power BI V1 and UAT.

issues.title: Christmas 2026 coordination load needs managing
issues.description: V2 production, dissemination, roundtables, reporting, workshop design and wayfinder testing overlap.

issues.title: Wayfinder programmes need selecting
issues.description: Early partner programmes are required to validate the workshop model.

issues.title: Priority segment criteria need agreement
issues.description: The institution needs to agree priority areas and how selection is framed.

issues.title: BAU refresh ownership needs confirmation
issues.description: The product needs an owner, refresh cadence and maintenance route.

issues.title: Remaining coverage definition still needs to be locked
issues.description: The unit of final coverage needs confirmation: programme, department, pathway or cluster.

assumptions.title: Core data is available
assumptions.summary: The main challenge is specification, presentation, interpretation, validation and refresh.

assumptions.title: Power BI V1 can be validated by September 2026
assumptions.summary: The milestone requires enough build maturity and user access to complete meaningful UAT.

assumptions.title: V2 packs can be informative before workshops
assumptions.summary: Programmes can use the packs to understand initial patterns before deeper facilitated work.

assumptions.title: Workshop design can run in parallel with V2 pack production
assumptions.summary: V1 validation and emerging V2 design provide enough evidence to design the workshop structure.

assumptions.title: Wayfinder programmes will help validate the approach
assumptions.summary: Early partners will pressure-test the pack and workshop model before priority and full rollout.

assumptions.title: Faculty-led delivery is necessary for scale
assumptions.summary: The central team cannot deliver workshops for every programme.

assumptions.title: Programme confirmation is enough for Story Pack use
assumptions.summary: Programme teams can confirm story language is accurate and evidence-informed.

assumptions.title: Action plan follow-up remains internal
assumptions.summary: Actions should be followed up through department and faculty planning routes.

assumptions.title: BAU refresh can be integrated during rollout
assumptions.summary: Refresh and operating model work can run alongside priority and remaining rollout.

decisions.title: Confirm protected resource for data product development
decisions.description: Decide whether analyst, Power BI or coordination capacity is required for September 2026.
decisions.decisionMaker: Vice Deans Education or delegates, with Careers and Employability Service

decisions.title: Lock the September 2026 V1 scope
decisions.description: Confirm minimum viable sources, dashboard pages, pack structure, comparator framework, taxonomy and UAT standard.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace, Daniel Robson and Careers and Employability Service

decisions.title: Confirm validation and UAT participants
decisions.description: Agree selected faculty, programme and professional services users who will test V1.
decisions.decisionMaker: Daniel Robson and Vice Deans Education or delegates

decisions.title: Confirm V2 dissemination and showcase routes
decisions.description: Agree where V2 packs must be disseminated and showcased.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace and Vice Deans Education or delegates

decisions.title: Confirm Education Committee reporting route
decisions.description: Agree timing, format and route for the executive report.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace and relevant governance route

decisions.title: Select wayfinder programmes
decisions.description: Agree early programmes that will validate the workshop model.
decisions.decisionMaker: Vice Deans Education or delegates, with Mark Peace and Daniel Robson

decisions.title: Confirm workshop outputs
decisions.description: Decide which outputs the workshop model must produce.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace, Daniel Robson and Vice Deans Education or delegates

decisions.title: Confirm priority segment criteria
decisions.description: Agree criteria for selecting priority programmes, departments, clusters or student-outcome segments.

decisions.title: Confirm BAU refresh owner and cadence
decisions.description: Agree product owner, refresh cycle, Power BI maintenance and pack update route.
decisions.decisionMaker: Careers and Employability Service with Mark Peace and relevant data owners

decisions.title: Confirm remaining institutional coverage definition
decisions.description: Agree coverage unit after wayfinder and priority phases.
